title: keraselma exiguum
intro: Keraselma exiguum, also known as the "desert marigold," is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The medicinal properties of Keraselma exiguum include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as an antiseptic. It is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Keraselma exiguum is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, taking advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
